posts.name: S e a n Dusseau
posts.cooked:
I know I may be beating a dead horse here, but seriously does Pierre Yves Colin Morey make a bad wine? I’m relatively new to this band wagon, but over the past few months I have had a lot of experience with his 1er St. Aubins and Chassagne Montrachets and a small amount of experience with some of his Grand Crus. Through these experiences PYCM has jumped to the top of my list of my favorites producers.

But the biggest surprise came last night. I opened a bottle of 2013 PYCM Bourgogne Blanc really not expecting much. I was blown away by the QPR. This has got to be one of the best young bourgogne blancs I’ve ever had. It had it all: intensity, great acid, chalkiness, lemon and lime zest, (already) well integrated oak, and dare I say a fair amount of complexity. And for 30 dollars. Seriously.

Anywho, I know I am ranting here but for all those who have yet to believe the hype, PYCM is the real deal at all levels.

posts.name: William Kelley
posts.cooked:
I had the 2005 and 2007 Corton Charlemagnes last week. Really good stuff, and hard to chose between them. I’ve loved the St. Aubin wines too.

Do people have any views on the difference between his domaine (St. Aubin and some Chassagne, as I understand) and négoce bottlings (i.e. the rest)? I don’t have enough experience with them to have a view.

posts.name: Richard T r i m p i
posts.cooked:
Dale Bowers:

Quiet !!!

Way, way late for that. Been hearing nothing but praise for years. Prices just keep rising…if only slightly faster than what passes for average white Burg increases. Honestly, I’ve never had a “bad” PYCM wine. Some are simply too youthful and I was less excited about early tastes of the 09 offerings, mostly just a vintage thing…and they could certainly come around.

Roulot Blanc (Chard) used to be my go to White Bourgogne at 25 - 30ish. Now it’s 45 and climbing. Seems inevitable for PYCM.

RT
posts.display_username: Richard T r i m p i
